statement.markdown: Given an empty max heap and a list of integers, implement a function to insert all elements into the heap and then print the maximum element from the heap after each insertion. If the heap becomes full, it should be rotated. The function should output the maximum element after each insertion and the final maximum element in the heap. If the input list is empty, the function should output an empty list.
statement.inputFormat: Input will consist of a list of integers and the size of the heap (n).
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a list of maximum elements after each insertion and the final maximum element in the heap.
statement.notes: The heap size (n) will be between 1 and 10,000. The input list will contain integers between 0 and 10,000.

examples.explanation: Insert all elements into the heap and print the maximum element after each insertion.

hints.text: Check the logic of the heap insertion process.
Make sure the heap is being rotated correctly.

editorial.title: Correct Heap Insertion
editorial.intuition: To correctly insert into a max heap, start at the last leaf node and then work our way up the tree, swapping nodes until we reach the root.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to simply insert all elements into the heap and then print the maximum element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a max heap data structure and insert elements in the correct position.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the properties of a max heap.
editorial.dryRun: First, we start with an empty max heap and a list of integers.
commonMistakes: Not checking if the heap is full before inserting a new element.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<int> &A, int n) { int heap[10] = {0}; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { heap[i % 10] = A[i]; cout << "Maximum element after insertion " << max_element(heap, heap + 10) << endl; } return vector<int>(); } }
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public List<Integer> solve(List<Integer> A, int n) { int[] heap = new int[10]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { heap[i % 10] = A.get(i); System.out.println("Maximum element after insertion " + Arrays.stream(heap).max().getAsInt()); } return Collections.emptyList(); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, A: List[int], n: int) -> List[int]: heap = [0] * 10; for i in range(n): heap[i % 10] = A[i]; print("Maximum element after insertion", max(heap)); return [];
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(A, n) { var heap = new Array(10).fill(0); for (var i = 0; i < n; i++) { heap[i % 10] = A[i]; console.log("Maximum element after insertion", Math.max.apply(null, heap)); } return []; }

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, A: List[int], n: int) -> List[int]:

solutions.python: import sys import heapq class Solution: def solve(self, A: List[int], n: int) -> List[int]: A = [int(x) for x in sys.stdin.readline().split()]; heap = []; res = []; for i in range(n): heap.append(A.pop()); heap.sort(reverse=True); res.append(heap[0]); while A: heap[0] = A.pop(); heapq.heapify(heap); res.append(heapq.nlargest(1, heap)[0]); return res; def main(): n = int(sys.stdin.readline()); print([x for x in solution.solve(list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())), n)]); sys.stdin.readline();
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> #include <vector> #include <algorithm> using namespace std; class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<int> &A, int n) { vector<int> res; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { cout << "Maximum element after insertion " << *max_element(A.begin(), A.end()) << endl; if (i == n - 1) res.push_back(*max_element(A.begin(), A.end())); } return res; } }; int main() { int n; cin >> n; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cin >> n; Solution solution; vector<int> A(n); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cin >> A[i]; auto res = solution.solve(A, n); for (auto v : res) cout << v << endl; }
